name: Noise POP
description: The Noise POP affects every point with a noise field, which can be thought of as smooth, randomly-rising and falling values in 3D space. By default the Noise POP moves every point's P attribute a distance in X, Y and Z based on the point's position in the noise space. You can visualize the field by sending a [[Grid POP]] into a Noise POP. Turn on the toggle parameter Compute Point Normals to see proper shading. The Type menu lets you choose between Simplex, Perlin in 2-4 dimensions. Simplex has the least grid-like field appearance. The Period determines the approximate distance between two peaks in each dimension (if you set Harmonics to 0). Harmonics adds more detail by adding to the field another half-period noise field, and recursively for the number of "harmonics". Harmonic Spread is the ratio of period of one applied harmonic to the next. Furthermore the entire noise field can be translated, rotated or scaled via the Transform page. The noise field value averages 0 in X, Y and Z. The Amplitude and Exponent parameters affect the magnitude of the resulting value, and the Offset is added to the resulting noise values. '''Output attributes''' - On the Output page, you can output and name the raw noise values to an attribute named Noise by default. Because the Noise Size menu parameter on the Noise page defaults to Scalar, the Noise attribute is a single scalar float. If the Noise Size menu parameter is set to Vector, the Noise attribute is a 3-component float, or 4 if the Parameter Size is set to 4. On the Output page you can also output several math functions that are derived from the noise field, like Gradient and Curl. The Gradient is the rate-of-change of a scalar noise around each point in X Y and Z (aka the "first order derivative") and is a float3 named NoiseGradient Curl is an expression of how much the slope twists around each point. A 3D Curl is a float3 named NoiseCurl. A 2D Curl is float2 named NoiseCurl2 If Combine Operation is Add (default) or Multiply, a float3 noise is always applied to P, even though the noise may be set to scalar. With Combine Attribute Scope, the noise can be applied to any scalar or vector attribute (one component to 4 components), and output to any new or existing attribute. With Combine Entity you can instead combine the NoiseCurl with any attribute. With Noise Size set to Vector, changing the Parameters Size to 2, 3 or 4 gives you individual parameter controls of Amplitude, Exponent and Offset per X Y Z W dimension. To animate the noise, put something like absTime.seconds in any of the Translate or Translate 4D parameters on the Transform page. '''Per-point mapping of parameters''' - The Noise POP has a Map page, which allows every point to get a different value for Amplitude, Offset, Exponent, Period, Harmonic Spread and Harmonic Gain parameters. In this mechanism, a separate attribute in the input contains values that override (or add to / multiply by) the parameter value. See [[Mapping POP Attributes to Parameters]]. See also [[Random POP]].

parameters.name: Noise Lookup Attribute
parameters.label: Noise Lookup Attribute
parameters.description: Point input attribute to use when computing the noise.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: Determines the noise type.

parameters.name: Noise Size
parameters.label: Noise Size
parameters.description: Number of noise components to compute.

parameters.name: Seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Numerical value that initializes the randomization.

parameters.name: Period
parameters.label: Period
parameters.description: Period (scale) of the noise field.

parameters.name: Harmonics
parameters.label: Harmonics
parameters.description: The number of higher frequency components to layer on top of the base frequency. 0 harmonics give the base shape.

parameters.name: Harmonic Spread
parameters.label: Harmonic Spread
parameters.description: The factor by which the frequency of a harmonic increases relative to the previous harmonic.

parameters.name: Harmonic Gain
parameters.label: Harmonic Gain
parameters.description: Amplitude of the Harmonics layered on top of the base frequency.

parameters.name: Parameter Size
parameters.label: Parameter Size
parameters.description: Number of independent configurable parameter values.

parameters.name: Amplitude
parameters.label: Amplitude
parameters.description: The noise values amplitude (a scale on the values output).

parameters.name: Exponent
parameters.label: Exponent
parameters.description: Sets the exponent. The internal value is raised by the power of the exponent

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: Adds an offset to the resulting value.

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Sets the overall transform order for the transformations.

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description: Translate the points through the noise space.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: Rotate the points around the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. Angles are given in degrees.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: These three fields scale the Source geometry in the three axes.

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description: The pivot point for the transform rotates and scales.

parameters.name: Translate 4D
parameters.label: Translate 4D
parameters.description: Translates the points through the 4th noise dimension.

parameters.name: Noise
parameters.label: Noise
parameters.description: Enable the addition of the noise attribute.

parameters.name: Gradient
parameters.label: Gradient
parameters.description: Whether to output the noise gradient.

parameters.name: Curl 3D
parameters.label: Curl 3D
parameters.description: Whether to output the 3D curl of the noise.

parameters.name: Curl 2D
parameters.label: Curl 2D
parameters.description: Whether to output the 2D curl of the noise.

parameters.name: Combine Operation
parameters.label: Combine Operation
parameters.description: Specify how to combine the output value with the combine attribute value.

parameters.name: Combine Entity
parameters.label: Combine Entity
parameters.description: Specify which computed value to use for the combine operation.

parameters.name: Combine Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Combine Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Input attribute scope for the combine operation.

parameters.name: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Output Attribute Scope
parameters.description: Name of attribute to output (can choose components of attribute), can choose from menu.

parameters.name: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.label: Override Automatic Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to override the kind of attribute automatically created based on the POP input and parameters. Allows to specify manually the type and number of components of the new attribute.

parameters.name: Attribute Type
parameters.label: Attribute Type
parameters.description: The output attribute's data type, default float.

parameters.name: Components
parameters.label: Components
parameters.description: The number of components in the new custom attribute.

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: Default values of the output attribute components if they cannot be computed.

parameters.name: Compute Point Normals
parameters.label: Compute Point Normals
parameters.description: Whether to compute point normals as a post operation.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Selects the shape of the field.

parameters.name: Mapping
parameters.label: Mapping
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for attribute-to-parameter mapping.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Noise_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
